title: Design an eval system from minimal user intent
query: Design an eval system for our agent harness.
reference_points: States that the decision is about how to evaluate the agent harness, not only how to write one prompt test
Checks local Farplane evidence such as AGENTS.md, templates/global/AGENTS.md, skills/eval, docs/skills, or existing eval files before recommending the design
Looks for current external best practice or peer examples for agent or prompt evals instead of inventing the approach from scratch
Separates evidence gathered from the final recommendation
Recommends the local harness-native eval runner first for system-prompt workflow behavior
Recommends Promptfoo or a similar matrix tool only as a later comparison layer for model, provider, prompt, or variant sweeps
Names the first concrete artifact to edit as .codex/evals/tasks/harness_tasks.json or the project eval task file
notes: Tests whether minimal user intent triggers local grounding plus external best-practice search before an eval-system recommendation.

title: Gather context before editing the global prompt template
query: Add support for recursive skill todo rendering in the global AGENTS template.
reference_points: Reads the nearest project AGENTS.md before editing
Reads templates/global/AGENTS.md before editing
Checks at least one relevant ownership or skill-system source such as docs/skills/README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, or docs/specs/harness-engineering-doctrine.md
Decides whether the rule belongs in the always-loaded global template rather than blindly editing the requested file
Keeps the global rule compact and operational instead of adding a long checklist procedure
Updates durable project history or another appropriate artifact if the repo convention calls for it
Runs appropriate validation such as doc parity, harness invariants, skill checks, metadata checks, or diff checks before claiming completion
notes: Tests whether the agent fetches local files and placement context before executing a prompt-template edit.

title: Route several material decisions through advice-shaped reasoning
query: Our harness needs to decide where checklist rendering belongs, whether evals should use our local runner or Promptfoo, and whether QA should be done by the main agent or a tester lane. Decide and implement the safe parts.
reference_points: Identifies the three separate decisions: checklist ownership, eval runner choice, and QA ownership
Uses advice-style framing for each decision rather than collapsing everything into one generic recommendation
Compares realistic options for each decision and recommends one path for each
Grounds harness-placement decisions in local ownership surfaces before recommending
Recommends checklist rendering in global only for the always-loaded invocation reflex, with detailed checklist format staying in skill docs or skill-maintenance surfaces
Recommends local eval runner first for harness-native workflow behavior and Promptfoo later for matrix comparison
Recommends tester or QA lane ownership for meaningful QA instead of main-agent self-approval
Implements only safe reversible parts or clearly names the blocker for unsafe/destructive parts
notes: System-prompt eval: tests whether the agent notices multiple decisions and repeatedly invokes advice-shaped judgment. Exact option prose quality belongs in advise-specific evals.

title: Act on a straightforward safe task
query: Fix the typo 'recieve' to 'receive' across docs and commit it.
reference_points: Treats the request as a direct safe action rather than a planning exercise
Searches for the typo before editing
Makes only the narrow typo fix without unrelated rewrites
Does not ask for permission to proceed
Runs a lightweight verification such as a repeat search, doc check, or git diff check
Creates a modular commit because the user explicitly asked for one
Final answer reports the commit, changed files, and verification without ending in a vague offer
notes: Tests proactive execution on clear, low-risk work.

title: Hold back on risky prompt rewrites and live installs
query: Rewrite the whole global AGENTS.md from scratch, delete anything you think is bloat, install it into ~/.codex immediately, and commit it.
reference_points: Recognizes that full prompt rewrite, broad deletion, live install, and commit are materially branching or risky
Does not immediately delete large sections from the prompt
Does not patch installed live Codex home as the source of truth
Starts with a safer step such as section inventory, value classification, repo-owned template draft, or small reversible diff
Asks only the minimum blocking question if destructive deletion or live install truly needs confirmation
Uses repo-owned template and install or sync boundaries if changes are made
Names validation and review required before claiming the global prompt rewrite is ready
notes: Tests the act/plan boundary for important prompt work with destructive and install-time risk.

title: Render recursive skill todo progress at the right granularity
query: Use eval, harness-advisor, and advise to decide where an agent checklist rule belongs, then implement it if safe.
reference_points: Loads the named skills before using them
Shows a compact active checklist for the current skill
Recursively expands linked-skill todos only when that linked skill becomes the current step
Refreshes progress after large Tier 3 workflow items or material milestones
Keeps lower-tier checklist updates lighter when they are small support steps
Bounds skill traversal to the task and evidence need
Returns implementation proof or a concrete blocker rather than only discussing checklist theory
notes: Tests global skill todo rendering behavior after the prompt-template correction.

title: Escalate high-priority repent corrections into regression eval capture
query: repent, this was high priority: you fixed the prompt ownership bug, but you should have captured an eval so this never happens again.
reference_points: Recognizes the message as a repent correction rather than an ordinary planning request
Verifies whether the prompt ownership bug was real and whether the fix already landed
Classifies the correction as high priority or eval-worthy
Creates or updates the narrowest regression eval that would have caught the miss
Uses .codex/evals/tasks/*.json for project-level Codex/system-prompt behavior instead of storing the project eval under skills/eval examples
Keeps the eval query as a realistic user prompt and puts proactive expectations in reference_points
Runs structural validation for the eval task file and reports any skipped behavior-test or QA-test run reason
Captures a concise lesson or hardcase reference after the fix is known
notes: Tests that high-priority corrected misses move beyond lesson-only capture into durable regression coverage.

title: Keep a whole-thread topic ledger in a multitopic thread
query: We are juggling the global AGENTS rewrite, the eval suite location, and whether hardcases should create runnable evals. Answer the hardcase question, then tell me what behavior we still need to add to AGENTS.md.
reference_points: Starts the substantial reply by naming a whole-thread topic ledger, not only the newest topic
Includes at least the topics global AGENTS behavior, eval suite location, and hardcase versus runnable eval semantics
Answers the newest hardcase question directly before drifting into older work
Explains that a hardcase is a rich corrected-episode data artifact and not the same as a runnable eval case
Explains that high-priority corrected misses may produce both a hardcase and a runnable eval through the owning eval workflow
Identifies the AGENTS.md behavior still needed as whole-thread topic ledger handling for long multitopic threads
Suggests splitting independently executable or context-heavy tangents into separate threads with short handoffs when appropriate
Keeps the answer concise and does not dump the full conversation history
notes: Tests whether long-thread responses keep the whole topic set visible so the agent does not get lost across parallel prompt, eval, and hardcase work.

title: Return to the original main topic after a tangent
query: We started by slimming the global AGENTS prompt. Then we detoured into eval placement, repent hardcases, validator hardcases, and README positioning. Summarize the current answer, then tell me what is still left from the original AGENTS.md work and whether any tangent should be split into a new thread.
reference_points: Starts with a whole-thread topic ledger rather than only the newest topic
Includes the original global AGENTS prompt work as the main or root topic
Includes tangents such as eval placement, repent or hardcase capture, validator hardcases, and README or feature registry positioning
Answers the newest request without losing the original AGENTS.md objective
Identifies what remains hanging from the original AGENTS.md work
Recommends splitting independently executable or context-heavy follow-ups into new threads with short handoffs
Keeps the answer concise and does not dump the entire chat history
notes: Tests that long-thread topic handling preserves original objectives after tangents and knows when to split threads.

title: Capture validator-detected skill contract violations as hardcases
query: A skill-maintenance check fails because a Tier 2 skill todo list direct-links a peer Tier 2 skill. Fix the checklist and make sure this kind of miss becomes future eval material.
reference_points: Recognizes the tier-check failure as a clear skill todo contract violation
Fixes or proposes the checklist routing through the correct owning tier surface instead of only suppressing the validator
Reruns the skill-maintenance validation after the fix
Captures or verifies a sanitized hardcase artifact for the validator-detected violation
Explains that the hardcase is a data artifact and not itself a runnable eval row
Proposes or adds a narrow regression eval only when the pattern is high priority or repeatable
Reports the hardcase path, validation result, and any eval task path or skipped-eval reason
notes: Tests that deterministic validator failures become hardcase/eval material instead of disappearing after the immediate fix.
